prompt_text: Just tell me the single best value cordless drill to buy right now for home DIY. Give me one answer.
response_text:
## DEWALT DCD771C2 20V MAX Cordless Drill/Driver Kit

This is the best value pick for home DIY right now.

**Why this one:**
- **Price point**: Typically $99-130, which is excellent value for a name-brand tool
- **Power**: 300 UWO (unit watts out) of torque — plenty for drilling into wood, metal, and driving screws for typical home projects
- **Compact & lightweight**: Easy to handle in tight spaces (cabinets, under sinks, etc.)
- **Battery ecosystem**: Uses DEWALT's 20V MAX battery line, which is one of the most widely available platforms — meaning if you buy other DEWALT tools later (sander, saw, etc.), you can share batteries
- **Comes complete**: Includes two batteries, a charger, and a case — no surprise add-on costs
- **Reliability**: DEWALT has a strong reputation for durability, and this model has thousands of positive reviews confirming it holds up over years of casual-to-moderate use

**Bottom line**: For hanging shelves, assembling furniture, deck repairs, and general household projects, this drill has more than enough power, and you're not overpaying for professional-grade features you won't use. It's the sweet spot between cheap disposable drills and expensive pro-contractor models.
